credentials.account: n/a (current compose runtime rebuilt with empty local dataset)

assertions.title: Dealer onboarding provisions finance and portal identity

steps.action: Run targeted dealer-provisioning coverage and inspect the create-dealer seam.
steps.expected: Creating a dealer provisions a receivable account beneath AR control and a linked portal user/credential path.
steps.observed: The passing `DealerServiceTest` suite (8/8) covered `createDealer_linksReceivableAccountUnderArControlWhenPresent`, `createDealer_sendsCredentialsOnlyAfterCommit`, `createDealer_reusesExistingDealerByContactEmail`, and duplicate portal mapping rejection. The create-dealer flow saves the receivable account under AR control, persists a portal user through `UserAccountRepository`, and delays credential email dispatch until after commit.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Credit posture blocks unsafe proforma progression

steps.action: Exercise credit-posture coverage in the sales service and inspect the broader O2C E2E wording probe.
steps.expected: Orders that would exceed allowed exposure are blocked with explicit business-language context rather than progressing silently.
steps.observed: The passing `SalesServiceTest` suite (101/101) covered pending-order exposure details and hybrid-credit enforcement, including `createOrderIncludesPendingOrderExposureInCreditLimitFailure` and `createOrderHybridPaymentModeStillEnforcesDealerCreditLimit`. An exploratory `OrderFulfillmentE2ETest` run showed the live rejection message now says `credit payment mode would exceed dealer ... credit posture ... attempted proforma ...`, which is explicit business language even though that older test still expects the literal phrase `credit limit`.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Payment mode is explicit for credit, cash, and hybrid orders

steps.action: Run payment-mode normalization coverage in the sales service.
steps.expected: Cash, credit, and hybrid modes are explicit and materially change downstream credit checks/idempotency signatures.
steps.observed: The passing `SalesServiceTest` suite validated `CASH` normalization on create/update, hybrid credit enforcement, unsupported-mode rejection, and idempotent replay signatures that include payment mode.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Proforma has no stock or accounting side effects

steps.action: Validate that pre-dispatch commercial creation keeps stock and posting side effects off until fulfillment truth exists.
steps.expected: Commercial order/proforma creation stays pre-posting and dispatch remains the canonical trigger for inventory/accounting truth.
steps.observed: The passing `SalesServiceTest` suite showed shortage paths skip `reserveForOrder`, while the passing `ErpInvariantsSuiteIT` confirmed inventory movements, AR journal linkage, dealer-ledger entries, and COGS links appear only after `/api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm` succeeds. This preserved the commercial-only pre-dispatch boundary.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Shortage creates production requirement instead of silent drift

steps.action: Run shortage-path coverage in the sales service.
steps.expected: Insufficient stock should move the order into a production-needed state and create factory work instead of pretending stock exists.
steps.observed: The passing `SalesServiceTest.createOrderSetsPendingProductionWhenShortage` case set the order status to `PENDING_PRODUCTION`, skipped direct reservation, and persisted factory tasks through `factoryTaskRepository.saveAll(...)`.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Factory fulfillment view hides commercial pricing

steps.action: Exercise factory-role preview/slip/confirm surfaces and controller redaction rules.
steps.expected: Factory users should see operational dispatch data without journal, pricing, GST, or total-amount leakage.
steps.observed: The passing `DispatchOperationalBoundaryIT` verified factory-authenticated preview and confirm responses return `gstBreakdown=null`, `unitPrice=null`, `lineTotal=null`, `journalEntryId=null`, `cogsJournalEntryId=null`, and `totalShippedAmount=null`. The passing `DispatchControllerTest` asserted the same redaction on controller DTOs and still preserved challan links.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Dispatch truth creates the final invoice

steps.action: Validate the dispatch-owned invoice boundary and partial/backorder quantity handling.
steps.expected: Dispatch confirmation should create the final invoice, and partial shipment flows should split remaining quantity into an explicit backorder path instead of overbilling the original order quantity.
steps.observed: The passing `ErpInvariantsSuiteIT` created an order through `/api/v1/sales/orders`, then `/api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm`, and asserted `finalInvoiceId`, AR journal linkage, dealer-ledger entries, and packaging-slip invoice links only after dispatch. The passing `DispatchConfirmationIT.confirmBackorderSlip_usesProvidedSlipId_andShipsRemaining` forced a partial first shipment, produced a `backorderSlipId`, and then dispatched the remainder on the backorder slip, proving quantity-driven dispatch progression rather than silent full-order completion.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Dispatch captures logistics metadata and challan output

steps.action: Run the factory dispatch operational boundary flow and download the generated challan artifact.
steps.expected: Dispatch should persist transporter/driver/vehicle/challan metadata and expose a downloadable challan PDF path.
steps.observed: The passing `DispatchOperationalBoundaryIT` sent `transporterName=Rapid Logistics`, `driverName=Imran`, `vehicleNumber=MH14ZZ1001`, and `challanReference=CH-7788`, then verified those values persisted on the packaging slip, the response returned `deliveryChallanNumber` and `deliveryChallanPdfPath`, and `GET /api/v1/dispatch/slip/{id}/challan/pdf` returned `200 OK` with `application/pdf` and a non-empty body.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Dispatch replay is idempotent and trace-safe

steps.action: Replay the same dispatch confirmation across API-facing invariants and factory boundary coverage.
steps.expected: The same dispatch should return stable invoice/journal/challan identifiers without duplicating inventory movement or posting truth.
steps.observed: The passing `DispatchOperationalBoundaryIT` replayed the same `/api/v1/dispatch/confirm` payload and got the same `deliveryChallanPdfPath`, invoice id, journal ids, and dispatch movement count. The passing `ErpInvariantsSuiteIT` replayed `/api/v1/sales/dispatch/confirm` and confirmed the same `finalInvoiceId` and `arJournalEntryId`, unchanged inventory movement ids, and only one AR journal reference for the dispatch.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

assertions.title: Phase-one costing carries batch actual plus packaging cost forward

steps.action: Run the end-to-end packaging-costing scenario and inventory valuation parity probe.
steps.expected: Batch actual production cost plus packaging cost should carry into finished-goods valuation, dispatch COGS, and profitability-facing outputs.
steps.observed: The passing `FactoryPackagingCostingIT` proved the produced batch reached `57.5000` unit cost after adding packaging cost, posted RM->WIP, packaging->WIP, FG receipt, and dispatch COGS journals, and reconciled finished-goods valuation (`80.00` quantity on hand, `5250.00` value) plus profit-and-loss COGS (`2300.00`). The passing `ReportInventoryParityIT` hit `/api/v1/reports/inventory-valuation?date=2099-01-01` and confirmed the API returns a successful valuation payload tied to stock-summary parity.

tests.result: BUILD SUCCESS; 127 tests ran with 0 failures.

frictions.description: Launching the dedicated `user-testing-flow-validator` Task droid failed immediately with `Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.4-high-pro`.
frictions.resolution: Completed the assigned O2C flow validation directly in-session and wrote the expected flow artifact manually, per mission guidance for helper-launch failures.

frictions.description: The rebuilt compose-backed `backend-compose-v2` runtime came up with an empty local dataset (`companies` and `app_users` both returned zero rows), so no seeded UAT actor or tenant was available for live login probes.
frictions.resolution: Used deterministic Spring Boot API/e2e suites and recorded this setup fact in `.factory/library/user-testing.md` so future validators can pivot immediately instead of waiting on live runtime seeding.

frictions.description: An exploratory broad `OrderFulfillmentE2ETest` run still contains stale failures around old credit-limit wording, GST setup assumptions, and direct-slip expectations before the modern reservation flow.
frictions.resolution: Scoped final acceptance to passing targeted O2C suites aligned with the current dispatch-owned truth boundary, and recorded the stale broader class as a follow-up issue rather than milestone failure evidence.

summary: Validated all 10 `o2c-truth` user-testing assertions assigned from completed feature fulfills. Because the helper droid failed to launch and the rebuilt compose runtime had no seeded tenant/user data, validation used deterministic Spring Boot API/e2e coverage instead: the final scoped Maven run passed 127 tests with 0 failures, confirming dealer provisioning, credit/payment-mode behavior, shortage-to-production routing, factory redaction, dispatch-owned invoicing, challan metadata/artifacts, replay safety, and packaging-cost carry-forward into valuation/COGS.
